policy.legacy_reader_rule: every non-current accepted value requires a migration record and regression test

migrations.reader: Treat absence as schema 1 only; reject every explicit value other than 1.
migrations.writer: Scaffolds and maintained repository manifests always emit schema = 1.
migrations.semantic_delta: No package semantics change; the discriminator makes future evolution fail closed.
migrations.user_action: Add schema = 1 as a top-level key.
migrations.retirement: Retain through the 0.x train; removal requires a major compatibility decision and corpus telemetry.

migrations.reader: Read explicit versions 1 and 2; never infer a missing version.
migrations.writer: New locks emit version 2; v1 objects requiring v2 resolution metadata are promoted on canonical write.
migrations.semantic_delta: Version 2 records source selectors, resolution strategies, tag policy, resolved refs, export hashes, and environment fingerprints.
migrations.user_action: Run a lock-producing gcpm operation and review the canonical version 2 diff.
migrations.retirement: Retain v1 reads through the 0.x train; remove only with an offline migrator and a release-note deprecation cycle.

migrations.reader: Read explicit versions 2 and 3; never infer a missing :version.
migrations.semantic_delta: Version 3 binds the current value hash encoding and deterministic task scheduling metadata.
migrations.user_action: Regenerate logs for archival provenance; replay may still consume explicit version 2 logs.
migrations.retirement: Retain v2 replay through the 0.x train; removal requires an authenticated log migrator and golden replay corpus.

migrations.reader: Read explicit binary versions 1 and 2 under the same GPK magic.
migrations.writer: Production exports always emit version 2, including a zero-count refs section.
migrations.semantic_delta: Version 2 adds a deterministic, sorted refs section; payload object encoding is unchanged.
migrations.user_action: Re-export a v1 bundle to obtain canonical v2; imports continue to accept v1.
migrations.retirement: Retain v1 reads through the 0.x train; removal requires a streaming re-export tool and fixture census.
